note: token_or_webform is a submodule of token_or that decorates Webform's webform.token_manager service so token_or's [a|b|"literal"] OR/fallback syntax works inside Webform, with special handling for anonymous [current-user:*] tokens. No UI, no config, no plugin type. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded); no site mutation.

evals.prompt: I have token_or installed but its [a|b|"default"] fallback tokens don't work inside my Webform email handler. Which module do I need and why?
evals.expected_output: Enable the token_or_webform submodule. Webform replaces tokens via its own webform.token_manager service (not the core token service token_or overrides), so token_or_webform decorates it to honour the | fallback syntax.

evals.prompt: How does token_or_webform integrate with Webform's token system?
evals.expected_output: It decorates the webform.token_manager service (decoration priority 50) with TokenOrWebformTokenManager, a subclass of WebformTokenManager that overrides replace().

evals.prompt: With token_or_webform, what happens to a token like [current-user:display-name|"Guest"] in a Webform email when the submitter is anonymous?
evals.expected_output: For anonymous users the current-user: candidate is stripped (so it doesn't render 'Anonymous') and the next candidate is used—here the literal 'Guest'.

evals.prompt: When token_or_webform processes a Webform text value containing a piped OR token that resolves to nothing, is the leftover [a|b] text kept or removed?
evals.expected_output: Removed—token_or_webform forces the 'clear' option on for piped text, so unresolved OR groups are stripped rather than left as literal [a|b].

evals.prompt: Does token_or_webform change how normal (non-piped) Webform tokens are replaced?
evals.expected_output: No—only text containing a | pipe is special-cased; text without | and array input pass straight through to the normal Webform token manager unchanged.

evals.prompt: What modules must be enabled for token_or_webform to work?
evals.expected_output: Both Webform (webform:webform) and Token Or (token_or:token_or); token_or_webform is a submodule of token_or.
